title: Unemployment Reload Options
description: What are the conditions and choices for reloading unemployment benefits in France after returning to work?
narrative: The user seeks to understand how unemployed individuals in France can regain or increase their unemployment benefits after returning to work, specifically focusing on the mechanisms of 'reloadable rights' and the 'right of option.' Relevant documents detail that reloadable rights allow benefit periods to be extended based on new employment (minimum 910 hours or 130 days since last claim), while the right of option permits claimants to switch to a new benefit calculation based on recent wages if it results in at least a 30% increase or a daily allowance of €20 or less. The user needs to distinguish between automatic reloads and the strategic choice offered by the right of option, including deadlines (21 days to decide), eligibility criteria, and consequences of each choice. Irrelevant documents discussing general job center structure, training allowances, or Wikipedia-style institutional history do not address these nuanced decision-making criteria.
